message:
feat(fsfs): add WAL-based incremental mutation commands (append-batch, delete, compact, daemon)

Introduce four new CLI commands that enable incremental index mutation
without requiring a full rebuild, all operating through a write-ahead log
(WAL) sidecar architecture:

- **append-batch**: Reads JSONL documents (from --file or stdin), embeds
  their text using the configured fast embedder, and appends the resulting
  vectors to the WAL. Each input line must contain "id" and "text" fields.

- **delete**: Soft-deletes documents by writing tombstone markers to the
  WAL. Supports both exact doc ID matching (positional args) and prefix
  matching (--prefix flag). Deleted documents are immediately excluded
  from search results and physically removed on next compaction.

- **compact**: Merges WAL entries into the main FSVI index file, then
  vacuums tombstoned records. Reports detailed stats including record
  counts before/after and timing for both compaction and vacuum phases.

- **daemon**: Long-lived process that polls the WAL sidecar file at a
  configurable interval (--poll-ms, default 1000ms) and automatically
  triggers compaction when new entries are detected. Integrates with the
  shutdown coordinator for graceful SIGINT handling.

CLI changes (cli.rs):
- Add four new CliCommand variants and their aliases (append/ab, del)
- Add CliInput fields: input_file, delete_ids, delete_prefix, daemon_poll_ms
- Register --file (append-batch only), --prefix (delete only), --poll-ms
  (daemon only) as command-specific flags with validation
- Parse positional doc IDs for the delete command before flag processing
- Validate that delete requires at least one document ID argument

Main entry point (main.rs):
- Extend shutdown-aware execution path to include Daemon command alongside
  Tui and watch mode

Runtime (runtime.rs):
- Import BufRead for stdin line iteration in append-batch
- Add command dispatch for all four new commands in the main execute path
- Implement run_append_batch_command (async): JSONL parsing, batch
  embedding, dimension validation, WAL append, structured output
- Implement run_delete_command: exact/prefix soft-delete, tombstone
  statistics, vacuum hints
- Implement run_compact_command: WAL merge + vacuum with detailed stats
- Implement run_daemon_command (async): WAL polling loop with configurable
  interval, automatic compaction on change detection, shutdown coordination

All commands support both Table (human-readable) and structured (JSON/JSONL)
output formats via the existing OutputEnvelope system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
